ux_decisions.decision: Center the MVP around a trip workspace with public page, private member workspace, and private event subspaces.
Treat external contribution checkout as a clear off-platform handoff.
Use scoped navigation with persistent trip context and nested private event context.
Use explicit confirmation before public publication or public media promotion.
Represent async work with pending, failed, retry, and manual fallback states.
Use screen groups aligned to public, private trip, private event, and admin scopes.
Specify launch-critical feature behavior by workflow group rather than isolated controls.
Represent workflow states explicitly in the interface instead of hiding backend or provider delays.
Treat accessibility as a baseline requirement for Stage 5, not an optional polish pass.

ux_risks.risk: Users may confuse external contribution status with Crewly-held funds.
ux_risks.mitigation: Use off-platform handoff language and avoid balance/custody labels for provider references.

ux_risks.risk: Nested private event context may be confused with trip-wide context.
ux_risks.mitigation: Use persistent event scope labeling and separate ledger summaries.

ux_risks.risk: Trip Ledger screen could become overloaded by expenses, contribution references, and event summaries.
ux_risks.mitigation: Separate sections and summaries; never merge event balances into trip totals.

ux_risks.risk: Public contribution setup could look like a native payout account flow.
ux_risks.mitigation: Use external provider target language and omit Crewly payout/account balance controls.

ux_risks.risk: Scope and privacy state could be communicated visually only.
ux_risks.mitigation: Expose scope text, ARIA labels, and consistent headings for public/private/event contexts.

interaction_tradeoffs.choice: Separate public, trip, and event scopes clearly even if navigation is less compact.

interaction_tradeoffs.benefit: Avoids misleading users about Crewly payment custody.

interaction_tradeoffs.cost: Requires more state-specific UI and implementation work.

answered_questions: Stage 3 defined public/private/event/admin scopes and role-based access.
assumptions_made: Stage 4 assumes a responsive web app screen system rather than separate native app flows.
Stage 4 assumes provider selection is deferred to Stage 5 and does not alter the external handoff pattern.
Stage 4 assumes all public exposure actions require explicit confirmation.
Stage 4 assumes baseline accessibility support is required for MVP.
user_confirmations: Use external provider option for contribution checkout and payout.
ux_confidence_gaps: Exact contribution provider return/sync behavior remains provider-dependent.
Visual density of ledger and receipt screens should be validated in design prototypes.
Specific notification channel behavior remains a Stage 5 provider/detail decision.

development_orchestration_inputs: Build implementation slices around screen groups and behavior groups.
Every sensitive workflow ticket needs permission, audit, state, and accessibility acceptance criteria.
External contribution provider implementation must use off-platform handoff and provider-reference terminology.
Receipt implementation must include async state, manual fallback, and source/display labeling.
Private event implementation must preserve participant-scoped navigation and separate ledger summaries.

completion_status.reason: Stage 4 UX outputs are complete and usable for development orchestration.
next_actions: Begin Stage 5 development orchestration using Stage 1 through Stage 4 outputs.

completion_status.reason: All six Stage 4 outputs exist, MVP journeys are mapped, screen system and behavior rules cover launch-critical workflows, state transitions and accessibility framework are defined, and high-risk UX items have mitigation paths.
next_actions: Begin Stage 5 development orchestration after audit passes.
